text: The following events were reported: Marine Thunderstorm Wind, Waterspout. These occurred near station USW00012894, approximately 36.1018 km away, between 2021-06-26 14:18:00 and 2021-06-26 14:23:00.Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Episode Narrative: Increased moisture associated with a mid-level disturbance moved westward across the South Florida as a result of easterly flow in place. As thunderstorms developed and pushed offshore into the Gulf of Mexico waters, a few of these storms resulted in gusty winds and a waterspout. Increased moisture associated with a mid-level disturbance moved westward across the South Florida as a result of easterly flow in place. Event Narrative: Trained spotter and several media partners reported a waterspout associated with severe thunderstorm that moved into the near-shore Gulf waters. Several pictures and videos indicate that the waterspout was located just along Bonita Shores and Barefoot Beach. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 40 mph / 35 knots was recorded by NCDC NOS station NPSF1 at Naples Pier. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 50 mph / 43 knots was recorded by the Earth Networks mesonet station NPLSR located at the Naples Grande Beach Resort in Vanderbilt Beach.

Increased moisture associated with a mid-level disturbance moved westward across the South Florida as a result of easterly flow in place. Event Narrative: Trained spotter and several media partners reported a waterspout associated with severe thunderstorm that moved into the near-shore Gulf waters. Several pictures and videos indicate that the waterspout was located just along Bonita Shores and Barefoot Beach. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 40 mph / 35 knots was recorded by NCDC NOS station NPSF1 at Naples Pier. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 50 mph / 43 knots was recorded by the Earth Networks mesonet station NPLSR located at the Naples Grande Beach Resort in Vanderbilt Beach.

The following events were reported: Marine Thunderstorm Wind, Waterspout. These occurred near station USW00012894, approximately 36.1018 km away, between 2021-06-26 14:18:00 and 2021-06-26 14:23:00.Thankfully, no injuries or fatalities were reported. No significant property or crop damage was reported. Several pictures and videos indicate that the waterspout was located just along Bonita Shores and Barefoot Beach. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 40 mph / 35 knots was recorded by NCDC NOS station NPSF1 at Naples Pier. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 50 mph / 43 knots was recorded by the Earth Networks mesonet station NPLSR located at the Naples Grande Beach Resort in Vanderbilt Beach.

As thunderstorms developed and pushed offshore into the Gulf of Mexico waters, a few of these storms resulted in gusty winds and a waterspout. Increased moisture associated with a mid-level disturbance moved westward across the South Florida as a result of easterly flow in place. Event Narrative: Trained spotter and several media partners reported a waterspout associated with severe thunderstorm that moved into the near-shore Gulf waters. Several pictures and videos indicate that the waterspout was located just along Bonita Shores and Barefoot Beach. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 40 mph / 35 knots was recorded by NCDC NOS station NPSF1 at Naples Pier. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 50 mph / 43 knots was recorded by the Earth Networks mesonet station NPLSR located at the Naples Grande Beach Resort in Vanderbilt Beach.

Several pictures and videos indicate that the waterspout was located just along Bonita Shores and Barefoot Beach. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 40 mph / 35 knots was recorded by NCDC NOS station NPSF1 at Naples Pier. A marine thunderstorm wind gust of 50 mph / 43 knots was recorded by the Earth Networks mesonet station NPLSR located at the Naples Grande Beach Resort in Vanderbilt Beach.
